Reproducible Research: Activity Monitoring

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(ggplot2)

# Read the data; ensure activity.csv is in your project folder
data <- read.csv("activity.csv")
data$date <- as.Date(data$date, format = "%Y-%m-%d")

# Calculate total steps per day, ignoring missing values
steps_per_day <- data %>%
    group_by(date) %>%
    summarize(total_steps = sum(steps, na.rm = TRUE))

# Plot histogram
ggplot(steps_per_day, aes(x = total_steps)) +
    geom_histogram(binwidth = 1000, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
    labs(title = "Total Steps Per Day", x = "Steps", y = "Frequency")

# Calculate Mean and Median
mean_val <- mean(steps_per_day$total_steps, na.rm = TRUE)
median_val <- median(steps_per_day$total_steps, na.rm = TRUE)


interval_avg <- data %>%
    group_by(interval) %>%
    summarize(avg_steps = mean(steps, na.rm = TRUE))

# Time series plot
plot(interval_avg$interval, interval_avg$avg_steps, type = "l", col="blue",
     main = "Average Daily Activity Pattern", xlab = "Interval", ylab = "Avg Steps")

# Identify the maximum interval
max_interval <- interval_avg[which.max(interval_avg$avg_steps), ]



total_na <- sum(is.na(data$steps))

# Fill NA values
imputed_data <- data
for (i in 1:nrow(imputed_data)) {
    if (is.na(imputed_data$steps[i])) {
        imputed_data$steps[i] <- interval_avg$avg_steps[interval_avg$interval == imputed_data$interval[i]]
    }
}

# New histogram with filled data
imputed_steps_per_day <- imputed_data %>%
    group_by(date) %>%
    summarize(total_steps = sum(steps))

ggplot(imputed_steps_per_day, aes(x = total_steps)) +
    geom_histogram(binwidth = 1000, fill = "darkgreen", color = "white") +
    labs(title = "Total Steps Per Day (Imputed Data)", x = "Steps", y = "Frequency")

new_mean <- mean(imputed_steps_per_day$total_steps)
new_median <- median(imputed_steps_per_day$total_steps)

# Create factor variable
imputed_data$day_type <- ifelse(weekdays(imputed_data$date) %in% c("Saturday", "Sunday"), "weekend", "weekday")

day_type_avg <- imputed_data %>%
    group_by(interval, day_type) %>%
    summarize(avg_steps = mean(steps))
## `summarise()` has regrouped the output.
## ℹ Summaries were computed grouped by interval and day_type.
## ℹ Output is grouped by interval.
## ℹ Use `summarise(.groups = "drop_last")` to silence this message.
## ℹ Use `summarise(.by = c(interval, day_type))` for per-operation grouping
##   (`?dplyr::dplyr_by`) instead.
# Panel plot
ggplot(day_type_avg, aes(x = interval, y = avg_steps)) +
    geom_line(color = "darkred") +
    facet_wrap(~day_type, ncol = 1) +
    labs(title = "Weekday vs. Weekend Activity", x = "Interval", y = "Steps")
